title: Finalize each fold from a cv-fit run and produce a pooled CV summary
summary: Finalize each fold run directory written by cv-fit: compute diagnostics and part-worth utilities for each fold using all post-warmup draws, then write a pooled cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv to run_dir/. The CV summary reports per-fold log joint posterior (lp__) for model comparison.
description: Processes every per-fold subdirectory written by `cv-fit`, applying `finalize` to each: loading the chain CSVs and writing diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv in-place using all post-warmup draws. After all folds are finalized, writes cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv at the top of run_dir with the per-fold log joint posterior (lp__) — the standard metric for comparing HB-Stan model configurations across a CV run.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Directory written by cv-fit. Subdirectories containing hb_fit_chain_*.csv files are treated as fold run directories and finalized in-place. diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv are written to each fold subdirectory. cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv are written to run_dir/.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

details:
`cv-finalize` is the post-processing counterpart to `cv-fit`. It walks the per-fold subdirectories of run_dir, identifies those that contain chain CSVs, and runs the same finalization logic as the standalone `finalize` command on each — producing diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv per fold using all post-warmup draws.

Once all folds are finalized, it assembles a pooled summary (cv_summary.json + cv_summary.csv) at the top of run_dir, reporting the mean log joint posterior (lp__) per fold. This per-fold lp__ is the standard metric for comparing HB-Stan configurations in cross-validation: higher lp__ indicates better posterior fit on the fold's training data.

Like the standalone `finalize`, this command does not touch the chain CSVs — it is purely a read-and-summarise step and can be re-run.
value:
On success:

- Each fold subdirectory receives diagnostics.json, summary.json, and part_worths.csv.
- run_dir/ receives cv_summary.json and cv_summary.csv with per-fold lp__ statistics.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, then event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: All post-warmup draws are used; adaptation draws are controlled by num_warmup at sampling time.
The per-fold lp__ in cv_summary is used for model comparison, not the absolute value of part-worths.
Use cv-fit-finalize to run both cv-fit and cv-finalize in one call.

see_also.description: Produces the per-fold chain CSVs that this command processes.
Runs cv-fit then cv-finalize in a single call.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Directory written by cv-fit containing per-fold subdirectories.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

artifacts.title: Per-fold part-worth utilities
artifacts.description: Posterior-mean part-worth matrix for each fold's training respondents. One file per fold subdirectory.

artifacts.title: Per-fold convergence diagnostics
artifacts.description: Per-parameter Rhat and ESS for each fold.

artifacts.title: Cross-validation summary
artifacts.description: Per-fold log joint posterior (lp__) for model comparison. Use these values to choose between competing model configurations.

fields.name: lp__mean
fields.description: Mean log joint posterior for this fold.

fields.name: lp__sd
fields.description: Standard deviation of lp__ across draws.
